# Plotly Python

## Source Record
| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| URL | `https://plotly.com/python/` |
| Owner | Plotly |
| Scope | Python graphing library overview with example coverage and positioning |
| Why it matters to nbadb | Plotly is a likely option for rich interactive charts in notebooks, dashboards, and exploratory analysis surfaces |

## Summary
The Plotly Python landing page presents Plotly.py as an open-source graphing library for interactive, publication-quality charts. It emphasizes breadth across common statistical and analytical chart types while keeping a Python-first interface.

## Key Points
- Plotly.py is described as free and open source.
- The page highlights interactive, publication-quality output as the main value proposition.
- It explicitly lists line, scatter, area, bar, error-bar, box, histogram, heatmap, subplot, multi-axis, polar, and bubble charts.
- The landing page points readers toward examples as the main learning path.
- The page also surfaces the broader Plotly ecosystem around application-building.

## nbadb Relevance
- Strong candidate for interactive dashboards over star-schema or aggregate outputs.
- Heatmaps, scatter plots, and subplots are useful for player, team, and shot-distribution analysis.
- Multi-axis and polar support could help with more advanced analytical storytelling.
- Fits well where hover interactions and shareable browser-based exploration matter more than minimal bundle size.

## Notable Sections
- Library overview
- Chart-type examples
- Open-source positioning
- GitHub contribution entrypoint
